angle: The weak-versus-gap boundary and the off-corpus floor in assessRequestQuality / confidence-scoring
The weak-versus-gap boundary and the off-corpus floor in assessRequestQuality / confidence-scoring
title: Add a single-hit isolation gap signal: refuse 'good' when the head is a lone above-floor hit with no corroborating second result
problem: assessRequestQuality (confidence-scoring.ts:433-485) only ever emits 'gap' at line 484, which is reachable solely when topScore < LOW_THRESHOLD (0.4) AND qualityRatio < 0.3. A single spurious off-corpus hit at cosine 0.78 (the benchmarked 'kubernetes' case, 029/benchmark-results.md:71,81) takes the line 476 'good' branch: topScore 0.78 >= HIGH_THRESHOLD 0.7, and because the only other results are even weaker the topMargin (line 466) is LARGE, so topMargin >= LARGE_MARGIN_THRESHOLD (0.15) passes and the gate cites a spurious match. The current margin signal is perverse here: an off-corpus query where every hit is noise produces a BIG #1-to-#2 gap precisely because the corpus has nothing relevant, which the code rewards as a dominant top hit. There is no notion of corroboration (no second independent above-threshold hit) anywhere in the function.
evidence: confidence-scoring.ts:471-484 (the good/weak/gap branch ladder); confidence-scoring.ts:466-468 (topMargin computed within-result-set, not vs a noise floor); confidence-scoring.ts:78 (TOP_DOMINANT_THRESHOLD 0.8) and :39 (HIGH_THRESHOLD 0.7); 029/benchmark-results.md:71,81 (kubernetes 0.78 good, identical across 4 models, unrelated Retention Cleanup doc); result-confidence-scoring.vitest.ts:397-406 (the only gap test uses an EMPTY result set, confirming no lone-false-hit coverage exists)
proposal: Before returning 'good', require corroboration for any non-dominant head: when topScore is between HIGH_THRESHOLD and TOP_DOMINANT_THRESHOLD (0.7-0.8, the band the kubernetes 0.78 hit lives in), demand a SECOND result whose resolveCalibrationScore is also >= HIGH_THRESHOLD before allowing the qualityRatio/topMargin good-path at line 476. A lone hit in this band with no above-threshold corroborator downgrades to 'weak' (still surfaced, just not cited). Genuinely strong queries (the aligned graph/memory/routing rows at 0.82-0.88, benchmark-results.md:62-66) all have multiple high-cosine hits and keep their 'good'. A truly dominant hit at >= 0.8 keeps the unconditional good at line 471 untouched. Gate behind a default-off SPECKIT flag mirroring the existing pattern in search-flags.ts so it earns promotion on the 029 fixtures before defaulting on.
benefit: Catches the exact benchmarked false-citation (lone off-corpus 0.78) without touching the aligned-query good verdicts or the absolute thresholds. Converts the false 'good'/cite_results into 'weak'/do_not_cite_results, which is the do-not-cite safety the envelope is supposed to provide. Cite-iff-good fidelity (1.0 in the benchmark) is preserved because the change only tightens what counts as good.
risk: A real single-hit query (corpus genuinely has exactly one strongly-relevant doc at 0.7-0.8) would downgrade to weak and lose its citation. Mitigation: the >= 0.8 dominant path (line 471) still fast-paths the clearest hits, and the corroboration rule only bites in the 0.7-0.8 band where a single nomic-baseline cosine is least trustworthy. Flag-gate and validate on the 029 12-query fixture set (aligned vs off-corpus) before default-on.

angle: The weak-versus-gap boundary and the off-corpus floor in assessRequestQuality / confidence-scoring
title: Subtract a measured corpus noise-floor from the absolute-relevance signal before banding, so the 0.7/0.4 thresholds mean relevance-above-baseline
problem: resolveAbsoluteRelevance (pipeline/types.ts:90-97) returns the raw normalized cosine with no noise-floor subtraction or per-corpus centering. nomic/sqlite-vec embeddings assign a high baseline cosine to ANY fluent English text, so a semantically-unrelated doc lands at 0.78 (029/benchmark-results.md:81 confirms this is 'a property of the absolute-relevance calibration and not a model behavior'). The 0.8/0.7/0.4 thresholds in confidence-scoring.ts (:39,:40,:78) are applied to this un-floored cosine, so the entire good/weak/gap ladder inherits the embedder's inflated baseline. 'authentication' correctly stays weak at 0.50 (benchmark-results.md:72) only because it happened to land below 0.7, not because any floor logic distinguishes it from kubernetes.
evidence: pipeline/types.ts:90-97 (resolveAbsoluteRelevance: pure normalize, no floor); 029/benchmark-results.md:81 ('property of the absolute-relevance calibration'); confidence-calibration.ts:8-18 (the isotonic model is explicitly UNVALIDATED INFRASTRUCTURE fit on a corpus-derived proxy, and confidence-scoring.ts:390-400 documents the band is taken off the PRE-calibration rebalancedValue so the existing isotonic curve cannot fix the band); confidence-scoring.ts:170-174 (resolveCalibrationScore is the single choke point both assessRequestQuality and computeResultConfidence read)
proposal: Introduce an empirically-measured off-corpus cosine floor (e.g. the median or 90th-percentile cosine of a held-out set of off-corpus queries against the live corpus, measured once and committed as data alongside confidence-calibration-model.json). Apply a floor-aware rescale in a new helper that resolveCalibrationScore (confidence-scoring.ts:170-174) calls when SPECKIT_ABSOLUTE_RELEVANCE_CALIBRATION is on: rescaled = max(0, (cosine - floor) / (1 - floor)). This shifts the kubernetes 0.78 below the floor toward weak/gap while leaving genuinely-relevant 0.85+ hits comfortably above 0.7. Ship the floor as committed data with the same lazy-load/mtime cache pattern already used for the calibration model (confidence-scoring.ts:182-210).
benefit: Fixes the root cause rather than the symptom: every threshold comparison downstream (band labels, request-quality, citation policy) becomes relevance-above-noise instead of raw-cosine. Both off-corpus rows (kubernetes and authentication) and any future off-corpus term get the same treatment deterministically. One choke point change propagates to all consumers.
risk: Requires measuring a real floor; a wrong floor either lets the false hit through (too low) or suppresses legitimate moderate hits (too high). The floor is corpus-dependent and drifts as the corpus grows, so it needs a re-measure cadence. Mitigation: 005 phase 020 already scopes an embedding-drift monitor (spec.md:203) that could trigger floor re-measurement; flag-gate and require the floor to be validated on the 029 off-corpus vs aligned split before default-on.

angle: The weak-versus-gap boundary and the off-corpus floor in assessRequestQuality / confidence-scoring
title: Encode the 029 off-corpus false-relevance cases as a red-team request-quality regression fixture
problem: The good/weak/gap thresholds (HIGH_THRESHOLD 0.7, LOW_THRESHOLD 0.4, TOP_DOMINANT_THRESHOLD 0.8, LARGE_MARGIN_THRESHOLD 0.15) in confidence-scoring.ts:39-78 are hand-set constants with no fixture that pins their off-corpus behavior. The only request-quality test (result-confidence-scoring.vitest.ts:359-407) asserts good on a 94-cosine pair, weak on a 57/55 pair, and gap on an EMPTY set. There is no regression test asserting that a lone off-corpus high-cosine hit does NOT read good, so any future threshold retune could silently re-open the false-citation without a failing test. The 029 benchmark found this by running 144 live dispatches, but that evidence is not encoded as a guarding fixture.
evidence: result-confidence-scoring.vitest.ts:359-407 (existing request-quality test: good/weak/gap but gap only via empty set, no off-corpus lone-hit case); 029/benchmark-results.md:60-73 (the per-query top-score+verdict table is a ready-made fixture source); 030/spec.md:67 (red-team eval class is named in-scope for the improvement research); confidence-scoring.ts:39-78 (the constants the fixture would pin)
proposal: Add a vitest fixture (alongside result-confidence-scoring.vitest.ts) that replays the 029 benchmarked cosine profiles: the kubernetes-style lone off-corpus hit (top ~0.78, weaker tail) must assert requestQuality != good / citationPolicy == do_not_cite_results once a fix lands, and the aligned graph/memory/routing profiles (0.82-0.88 with multiple high hits) must still assert good. Include the authentication 0.50 case as a passing weak control. This both guards the fix from proposals 1 and 2 and gives 005 the red-team eval class its 030 research scope already calls for (030/spec.md:67 lists 'a red-team eval set' as an in-scope angle).
benefit: Turns the one-time 144-cell benchmark finding into a permanent guard that fails CI if a threshold retune re-introduces the lone-off-corpus false-good. Low blast radius (test-only), and it is the prerequisite that lets proposals 1 and 2 be validated objectively rather than by eyeball.
risk: A fixture hard-codes specific cosine values; if the embedder is swapped the absolute numbers shift and the fixture needs re-derivation. Mitigation: assert on the qualitative verdict (good vs weak, cite vs do-not-cite) given a cosine profile, and source the profile from the 029 metrics so re-derivation is mechanical.

angle: The weak-versus-gap boundary and the off-corpus floor in assessRequestQuality / confidence-scoring
title: Bridge stage4.evidenceGapDetected into the formatter's request-quality verdict so the two gap signals reconcile
problem: Two separate request-quality derivations exist and they disagree on what 'gap' means. The pipeline/formatter path (search-results.ts:1086-1087 calls assessRequestQuality, :1161 derives citationPolicy from it) bands gap off topScore/qualityRatio. But the handler path (memory-search.ts:1325) sets requestQualityLabel = stage4.evidenceGapDetected ? 'gap' : 'weak' and feeds that to routeQuery for fallback routing (memory-search.ts:1326-1330). The handler's gap is driven by an evidence-gap detector that never sees the lone-false-hit case, while the formatter's gap (the one that drives citationPolicy the benchmark measured) ignores evidenceGapDetected entirely. The off-corpus false hit is invisible to BOTH: the formatter says good, and the handler's evidenceGapDetected is computed upstream of confidence scoring.
evidence: memory-search.ts:1325 (handler: requestQualityLabel from evidenceGapDetected, only gap-or-weak); memory-search.ts:1326-1330 (that label drives routeQuery/qualityFallback, a DIFFERENT path than citation); search-results.ts:1086-1087,1161,1176 (formatter: assessRequestQuality -> deriveCitationPolicy -> emitted citationPolicy, the one the 029 benchmark observed at cite-correct 1.0); search-results.ts:368-371 (deriveCitationPolicy is purely label === good ? cite : do_not_cite, no evidence-gap input)
proposal: Pass the pipeline's stage4.evidenceGapDetected (and/or the qualityGapFallback.engaged state) into formatSearchResults so deriveCitationPolicy can force do_not_cite_results when the evidence-gap detector fired, even if assessRequestQuality independently banded the lone hit as good. This makes the two gap notions a logical OR rather than two disconnected verdicts, closing the seam where a spurious hit passes the formatter band while the handler's gap router would have flagged it. Confirm first whether evidenceGapDetected actually fires for the kubernetes case (it may not, in which case this proposal is subsumed by proposals 1-2 and drops to P2).
benefit: Removes a structural blind spot where two gap detectors run but neither covers the lone-false-hit, and ensures any future evidence-gap improvement automatically tightens citation. Makes citationPolicy a function of all available gap evidence, not just the cosine band.
risk: If evidenceGapDetected does NOT actually fire on off-corpus false hits (likely, since it is computed before confidence scoring), this bridge adds wiring without fixing the benchmarked case, and the real fix stays in proposals 1-2. Must verify the detector's behavior on the kubernetes fixture before building. Lower priority than 1-2 because it is a defense-in-depth reconciliation, not the primary fix.
